title: Lee et al. 2019 (ERP) — EEG dataset and OpenBMI toolbox for three BCI paradigms: an investigation into BCI illiteracy
description: This EEG dataset comprises recordings from 54 healthy participants performing a P300-based copy-spelling task using a 36-symbol row-column speller paradigm. The dataset includes 62 EEG channels and 4 EMG channels sampled at 1000 Hz, with offline training and online test phases designed to investigate BCI illiteracy. The study achieved 96.7% accuracy and identified an 11.1% illiteracy rate among participants, providing a valuable resource for benchmarking brain-computer interface algorithms and understanding individual differences in BCI performance.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a 62-channel BrainAmp system with Ag/AgCl sensors at 1000 Hz sampling rate, referenced to nasion with ground at AFz. Participants performed a copy-spelling task with visual feedback, counting target character flashes in a row-column speller paradigm. Training involved spelling 'NEURAL NETWORKS AND DEEP LEARNING' (33 characters) and testing involved 'PATTERN RECOGNITION MACHINE LEARNING' (36 characters), with each character receiving 5 sequences of 12 flashes. Inter-stimulus interval was 135 ms with stimulus onset asynchrony of 215 ms.
